description: §48.3.3 — a reactive-variable write (`@counter = a`) inside a pure `fn` body SHALL fire E-FN-003. `@`-cells are program-scope reactive state; writing one from inside `fn` is an outer-scope mutation. Reject-path (pos) half of the E-FN-003 pin; the clean neg is `fn/reactive-read-clean` (read-only `@`-cell use — silent, §48.5.3).
rationale: §48.3.3: `fn` is a pure function and may not mutate reactive state; a `@cell = ...` write in a `fn` body is E-FN-003 (an outer-scope mutation — `@`-cells are always program-level). Move the write outside `fn`, or use `function` if the side effect is intentional.
